output.final_deliverable:
I visited the requested repository page (https://github.com/python/cpython). The visit returned a redirect/response pointing to GitHub support (content shown as: "https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer") and a list of generic GitHub links, so I could not read the repository page itself to extract the requested metadata.

What I could and could not extract from the visit: 
- Primary programming language: MISSING — the repository page content was not available in the visit results, so this field could not be read.
- Number of stars (approximate): MISSING — the star count was not available in the visit results.
- License type: MISSING — the license label was not available in the visit results.
- Brief description of the repository: MISSING — the repository description was not available in the visit results.

Evidence / citations:
- The attempted repository URL: https://github.com/python/cpython
- The returned content shown in the visit result: https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer
- Sample links captured in the visit result: https://github.com/, https://github.com/login?return_to=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fpython%2Fcpython, https://github.com/features/copilot

Next steps I can take if you want (requires another visit):
- Retry loading https://github.com/python/cpython and capture the repository page HTML/metadata.
- If GitHub is redirecting or blocking automated fetches, load the page in a browser session or use the GitHub API (api.github.com/repos/python/cpython) to retrieve the metadata.

If you want me to retry now, tell me which approach you prefer.
output.action_summary: Attempted to visit https://github.com/python/cpython but the visit returned/redirected to https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer and generic GitHub links; therefore I could not extract the primary language, star count, license, or description from the provided visit result. (Cited: https://github.com/python/cpython and https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer.)

result.title: Think: normalize and verify extracted fields from the GitHub page

details.intent: After the visit node returns raw text/snapshots, normalize the star count into an approximate integer, canonicalize the license label if possible, and check for missing pieces requiring follow-up visits.
details.processing_plan: 1) Normalize stars_text: parse formats like '55k', '3.2k', '1,234' into an integer approximation. 2) If license_label is abbreviated (e.g., 'PSF License' or 'MIT'), map to a canonical SPDX-like name when unambiguous. 3) If any target_fact is missing or ambiguous (e.g., license not visible), note that and produce recommended follow-up (visit LICENSE file link if present). 4) Produce a final structured result object containing the normalized fields plus the raw strings/snippets for verification.

measurable_outputs.missing_fields: array of strings (which facts, if any, are missing or ambiguous)

verification_steps: If normalization changes value (e.g., '55k' -> 55000), include both original text and normalized value in output.
If license is ambiguous, flag it and recommend visiting the LICENSE file link captured earlier.
details.rationale: Normalization and verification make the raw data actionable and highlight any need for additional data-gathering steps.

cf4ca4e2-9ff0-4c6e-bb37-bc8b9576444d.title: Visit the GitHub repository page for python/cpython and extract repository fields

details.intent: Directly load the repository main page to extract visible metadata (primary language, star count, license label, repository description) — user requires we visit the GitHub page itself, not rely on search results.
target_facts: primary_language (string) — the main language label shown on the repo page
stars_text (string) — the star count text as shown on the page (e.g., '55k')
stars_approx_integer (integer) — the approximate numeric star count derived from the text (e.g., 55000)
license_label (string) — license label shown near repo metadata, if present
description (string) — the short description of the repository displayed on the page
source_url (string) — the exact page URL visited
raw_page_snapshot (string) — snippet of relevant HTML/text extracted for verification
details.extraction_plan:
Load https://github.com/python/cpython. From the repository header extract: (a) the short description under the repository name (usually in a

or repository-about section); (b) the primary language label (often in the language pill or in the language bar on the repo file list); (c) the star count visible near the top (extract the text from the 'stargazers' button or star count anchor); (d) the license label shown in the repo metadata area (or a link to LICENSE). Capture the exact text strings as displayed and a small raw page snippet around each element for verification. Return both the raw strings and a normalized approximate integer for stars (apply normalization rules: 'k' -> *1000, 'M' -> *1,000,000; if plain number with commas, remove commas). If a license link to a LICENSE file is present, record the link URL (but do not automatically visit it in this node).
verification_steps: Include the exact star count text and a page snippet showing the star element for verification.
Include the exact license text or label visible on the repository page and the snippet around it; if a link to LICENSE exists, record its URL for possible follow-up verification.
Include the primary language pill/snippet so the user can confirm correctness.
details.rationale: Directly visiting the repository page is required by the user's mandate. This node reduces uncertainty by capturing the canonical, visible metadata presented on the repo home so the outputs are verifiable against the page.
details.parent_goal: Visit the GitHub repository at https://github.com/python/cpython and extract the following information: (1) The primary programming language used, (2) The number of stars (approximate), (3) The license type, (4) A brief description of what the repository is.
